docs.title: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog
docs.text:
LinkML schema for the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog. The KEV catalog is the authoritative source of vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild, maintained by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

URI: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

Name: kev-catalog

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Class Description KevCatalog The CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog — the authoritative so... Vulnerability A single entry in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, rep...

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Slot Description catalog_version The version string of the KEV catalog release count The total number of vulnerabilities in this catalog version cve_id The CVE identifier assigned to this vulnerability by a CVE Numbering Authorit... date_added The date (YYYY-MM-DD) the vulnerability was added to the KEV catalog date_released The ISO 8601 timestamp at which this catalog version was released due_date The date (YYYY-MM-DD) by which federal civilian executive branch (FCEB) agenc... known_ransomware_campaign_use Whether the vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part of a ransom... notes Additional notes or references related to the vulnerability or its remediatio... product The name of the affected product required_action The remediation action required, e short_description A brief description of the vulnerability title The title of the KEV catalog vendor_project The name of the vendor or project responsible for the affected product vulnerabilities The list of known exploited vulnerabilities vulnerability_name A short, descriptive name for the vulnerability

docs.title: Enumerations
docs.text: Enumeration Description KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum Whether the vulnerability is known to have been used in ransomware campaigns

docs.title: Types
docs.text: Type Description Boolean A binary (true or false) value Curie a compact URI CveId A CVE identifier in the format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN as assigned by a CVE Numbering ... Date a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar DateOrDatetime Either a date or a datetime Datetime The combination of a date and time Decimal A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal speci... Double A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification Float A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification Integer An integer IsoDate A date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) Jsonpath A string encoding a JSON Path Jsonpointer A string encoding a JSON Pointer Ncname Prefix part of CURIE Nodeidentifier A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model Objectidentifier A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model Sparqlpath A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path String A character string Time A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular... Uri a complete URI Uriorcurie a URI or a CURIE

docs.title: Type: CveId
docs.text:
A CVE identifier in the format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN as assigned by a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA).

URI: xsd:string

docs.title: Type Properties
docs.text: Property Value Base str Type URI xsd:string ## Value Constraints Property Value Regex Pattern ^CVE-[0-9]{4}-[0-9]+$

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • The first KEV inclusion criterion: the vulnerability must have an assigned CVE ID. The CVE Program is sponsored by CISA and operated by The MITRE Corporation.

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

docs.title: Class: KevCatalog
docs.text:
The CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog — the authoritative source of vulnerabilities confirmed exploited in the wild, maintained by CISA per Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01.

URI: kev_catalog:KevCatalog

 classDiagram
    class KevCatalog
    click KevCatalog href "../KevCatalog/"
      KevCatalog : catalog_version

      KevCatalog : count

      KevCatalog : date_released

      KevCatalog : title

      KevCatalog : vulnerabilities





        KevCatalog --> "*" Vulnerability : vulnerabilities
        click Vulnerability href "../Vulnerability/"

docs.title: Class Properties
docs.text: Property Value Tree Root Yes

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Cardinality and Range Description Inheritance title 0..1  String The title of the KEV catalog direct catalog_version 0..1  String The version string of the KEV catalog release direct date_released 0..1  String The ISO 8601 timestamp at which this catalog version was released direct count 0..1  Integer The total number of vulnerabilities in this catalog version direct vulnerabilities *  Vulnerability The list of known exploited vulnerabilities direct

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• BOD 22-01, "Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities", was issued under Section 3553(b)(2) of title 44, U.S. Code by the Secretary of Homeland Security. It is a compulsory direction to all FCEB departments and agencies.
  • CISA strongly recommends all organizations — including state, local, tribal and territorial (SLTT) governments and private sector — prioritize remediation of KEV catalog entries as part of their vulnerability management plan, even though BOD 22-01 itself only binds FCEB agencies.
  • BOD 22-01 does not apply to: national security systems (as defined by statute), systems operated by the Department of Defense, or systems operated by the Intelligence Community.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• https://www.cisa.gov/directives
  • https://www.cisa.gov/binding-operational-directive-22-01

docs.title: Enum: KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum
docs.text:
Whether the vulnerability is known to have been used in ransomware campaigns.

URI: kev_catalog:KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description known WIKIDATA:Q532924 The vulnerability is known to have been used in a ransomware campaign unknown None It is not known whether the vulnerability has been used in a ransomware campa...

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Description known_ransomware_campaign_use Whether the vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part of a ransom...

docs.title: Comments
docs.text: • CISA uses this field to signal whether the KEV entry has been observed as part of ransomware activity, enabling organizations to further prioritize their remediation efforts beyond the base BOD 22-01 timeline.

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

docs.title: LinkML Source
docs.text:
name: KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum
description: Whether the vulnerability is known to have been used in ransomware campaigns.
comments:
- CISA uses this field to signal whether the KEV entry has been observed as part of
  ransomware activity, enabling organizations to further prioritize their remediation
  efforts beyond the base BOD 22-01 timeline.
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  known:
    text: known
    description: The vulnerability is known to have been used in a ransomware campaign.
    meaning: WIKIDATA:Q532924
  unknown:
    text: unknown
    description: It is not known whether the vulnerability has been used in a ransomware
      campaign.

docs.title: Class: Vulnerability
docs.text:
A single entry in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, representing one CVE that has been confirmed exploited in the wild and for which clear remediation guidance exists.

URI: kev_catalog:Vulnerability

 classDiagram
    class Vulnerability
    click Vulnerability href "../Vulnerability/"
      Vulnerability : cve_id

      Vulnerability : date_added

      Vulnerability : due_date

      Vulnerability : known_ransomware_campaign_use





        Vulnerability --> "0..1" KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum : known_ransomware_campaign_use
        click KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum href "../KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum/"



      Vulnerability : notes

      Vulnerability : product

      Vulnerability : required_action

      Vulnerability : short_description

      Vulnerability : vendor_project

      Vulnerability : vulnerability_name

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Cardinality and Range Description Inheritance cve_id 1  CveId The CVE identifier assigned to this vulnerability by a CVE Numbering Authorit... direct vendor_project 1  String The name of the vendor or project responsible for the affected product direct product 1  String The name of the affected product direct vulnerability_name 1  String A short, descriptive name for the vulnerability direct date_added 1  IsoDate The date (YYYY-MM-DD) the vulnerability was added to the KEV catalog direct short_description 1  String A brief description of the vulnerability direct required_action 1  String The remediation action required, e direct due_date 1  IsoDate The date (YYYY-MM-DD) by which federal civilian executive branch (FCEB) agenc... direct known_ransomware_campaign_use 0..1  KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum Whether the vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part of a ransom... direct notes 0..1  String Additional notes or references related to the vulnerability or its remediatio... direct

docs.title: Usages
docs.text: used by used in type used KevCatalog vulnerabilities range Vulnerability

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• Three criteria must all be met for inclusion: (1) assigned CVE ID, (2) reliable evidence of active exploitation in the wild, and (3) clear remediation guidance (vendor update or EOL removal).
  • BOD 22-01 focuses on a small, high-signal subset of all vulnerabilities: as of 2022-07-15 the catalog contained 787 KEVs from a universe of ~179,383 CVEs, and approximately 20 of those KEVs were the most prevalent across the entire federal enterprise.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text: • https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog

docs.title: Mappings
docs.text: Mapping Type Mapped Value self kev_catalog:Vulnerability native kev_catalog:Vulnerability exact schema:SoftwareApplication, WIKIDATA:Q16798631

docs.title: Direct
docs.text:
name: Vulnerability
description: A single entry in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog,
  representing one CVE that has been confirmed exploited in the wild and for which
  clear remediation guidance exists.
comments:
- 'Three criteria must all be met for inclusion: (1) assigned CVE ID, (2) reliable
  evidence of active exploitation in the wild, and (3) clear remediation guidance
  (vendor update or EOL removal).'
- 'BOD 22-01 focuses on a small, high-signal subset of all vulnerabilities: as of
  2022-07-15 the catalog contained 787 KEVs from a universe of ~179,383 CVEs, and
  approximately 20 of those KEVs were the most prevalent across the entire federal
  enterprise.'
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
see_also:
- https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
exact_mappings:
- schema:SoftwareApplication
- WIKIDATA:Q16798631
slots:
- cve_id
- vendor_project
- product
- vulnerability_name
- date_added
- short_description
- required_action
- due_date
- known_ransomware_campaign_use
- notes

docs.title: Induced
docs.text:
name: Vulnerability
description: A single entry in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog,
  representing one CVE that has been confirmed exploited in the wild and for which
  clear remediation guidance exists.
comments:
- 'Three criteria must all be met for inclusion: (1) assigned CVE ID, (2) reliable
  evidence of active exploitation in the wild, and (3) clear remediation guidance
  (vendor update or EOL removal).'
- 'BOD 22-01 focuses on a small, high-signal subset of all vulnerabilities: as of
  2022-07-15 the catalog contained 787 KEVs from a universe of ~179,383 CVEs, and
  approximately 20 of those KEVs were the most prevalent across the entire federal
  enterprise.'
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
see_also:
- https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
exact_mappings:
- schema:SoftwareApplication
- WIKIDATA:Q16798631
attributes:
  cve_id:
    name: cve_id
    description: 'The CVE identifier assigned to this vulnerability by a CVE Numbering
      Authority (CNA). Format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - cveID
    exact_mappings:
    - schema:identifier
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: dcterms:identifier
    identifier: true
    alias: cve_id
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: CveId
    required: true
  vendor_project:
    name: vendor_project
    description: The name of the vendor or project responsible for the affected product.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - vendorProject
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:Organization
    alias: vendor_project
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: true
  product:
    name: product
    description: The name of the affected product.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - product
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:name
    alias: product
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: true
  vulnerability_name:
    name: vulnerability_name
    description: A short, descriptive name for the vulnerability.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - vulnerabilityName
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: dcterms:title
    alias: vulnerability_name
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: true
  date_added:
    name: date_added
    description: The date (YYYY-MM-DD) the vulnerability was added to the KEV catalog.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - dateAdded
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: dcterms:created
    alias: date_added
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: IsoDate
    required: true
  short_description:
    name: short_description
    description: A brief description of the vulnerability.
    comments:
    - 'KEV inclusion criterion #2 requires reliable evidence that the vulnerability
      has been actively exploited in the wild. Active exploitation includes attempted
      and successful exploitation; scanning, security research, and public PoC alone
      do not qualify.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - shortDescription
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: dcterms:description
    alias: short_description
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: true
  required_action:
    name: required_action
    description: The remediation action required, e.g. "Apply updates per vendor instructions."
    comments:
    - 'BOD 22-01 specifies two acceptable actions: (1) apply updates per vendor instructions,
      or (2) remove the product from agency networks if it is end-of-life or cannot
      otherwise be updated. Temporary mitigations and workarounds may be applied while
      awaiting a vendor patch.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - requiredAction
    rank: 1000
    alias: required_action
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: true
  due_date:
    name: due_date
    description: The date (YYYY-MM-DD) by which federal civilian executive branch
      (FCEB) agencies must remediate the vulnerability per BOD 22-01.
    comments:
    - Deadline set by CISA. Under BOD 19-02, internet-accessible critical vulnerabilities
      (CVSS >9.0) must be remediated within 15 calendar days and high vulnerabilities
      (7.0-9.0) within 30 calendar days of initial detection; BOD 22-01 due dates
      may be tighter and apply to all agency systems, not only internet-accessible
      ones.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - dueDate
    rank: 1000
    alias: due_date
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: IsoDate
    required: true
  known_ransomware_campaign_use:
    name: known_ransomware_campaign_use
    description: Whether the vulnerability is known to have been leveraged as part
      of a ransomware campaign.
    notes:
    - CISA encourages organizations with evidence of exploited vulnerabilities not
      listed in the KEV to submit them to vulnerability@mail.cisa.dhs.gov.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    aliases:
    - knownRansomwareCampaignUse
    rank: 1000
    alias: known_ransomware_campaign_use
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: KnownRansomwareCampaignUseEnum
    required: false
  notes:
    name: notes
    description: Additional notes or references related to the vulnerability or its
      remediation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: skos:note
    alias: notes
    owner: Vulnerability
    domain_of:
    - Vulnerability
    range: string
    required: false

docs.title: kev-catalog

LinkML schema for the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog. The KEV catalog is the authoritative source of vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild, maintained by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

URI: https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog
